title: Court Opinions Search — US Case Law to JSON
shortDescription: Search millions of US court opinions by keyword and get case name, court, date, docket number and citation count as JSON at $0.004 per opinion.
seoTitle: Court Opinions Search — US Case Law to JSON
seoDescription: Court opinions scraper & API: search US case law by keyword for case name, court, date and citation count as JSON. For legal and due-diligence AI agents.

query.label: Keyword, party, or topic
query.description: Term to search across US court opinions (CourtListener), e.g. a legal topic, party name, or case keyword.

readme.intro: Case law is where legal precedent and corporate disputes live. This actor turns a keyword into the matching US court opinions from CourtListener, so legal, compliance, and due-diligence agents can read case law as structured data — a natural companion to SEC filings and regulatory documents.
bullets: Search millions of US federal and state court opinions by keyword, party, or topic
Each result: case name, court, filing date, docket number, citation count, status, opinion URL
Filter by postedAfter for new-ruling monitoring
Batch many queries per run; cap spend with maxResults
Agent-ready: flat JSON with the opinion URL + scrapedAt for citation

sampleOutput.title: U.S. Office of Pers. Mgmt. Data Sec. Breach Litig.
sampleOutput.court: Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit

faq.a: US federal and state courts indexed by CourtListener (Free Law Project), spanning millions of opinions.

faq.q: How do I track new rulings?
faq.a: Set postedAfter to your last-run date and schedule it for an incremental case-law feed.
The CourtListener API by the Free Law Project — a public, non-profit legal database.

results.caseName: U.S. Office of Pers. Mgmt. Data SEC. Breach Litig. v. Office of Pers. Mgmt.
results.court: Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit

results.caseName: California Privacy Protection Agency v. Super. Ct.
